 16→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 17→
 18→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 19→import android.view.KeyEvent
 20→import androidx.core.content.edit
 21→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.keyCode
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.Companion.unicode
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.ctrl
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.Binding.ModifierKeys.Companion.shift
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.CardSide
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.*
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.fromPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.MappableBinding.Companion.toPreferenceString
 32→import java.util.*
 33→import java.util.function.BiFunction
 34→import java.util.stream.Collectors
 35→
 36→/** Abstraction: Discuss moving many of these to 'Reviewer'  */
 37→enum class ViewerCommand(val resourceId: Int) {
 38→    SHOW_ANSWER(R.string.show_answer),
 39→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1(R.string.answer_again),
 40→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2(R.string.answer_hard),
 41→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3(R.string.answer_good),
 42→    FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4(R.string.answer_easy),
 43→    UNDO(R.string.undo),
 44→    REDO(R.string.redo),
 45→    EDIT(R.string.cardeditor_title_edit_card),
 46→    MARK(R.string.menu_mark_note),
 47→    BURY_CARD(R.string.menu_bury_card),
 48→    SUSPEND_CARD(R.string.menu_suspend_card),
 49→    DELETE(R.string.menu_delete_note),
 50→    PLAY_MEDIA(R.string.gesture_play),
 51→    EXIT(R.string.gesture_abort_learning),
 52→    BURY_NOTE(R.string.menu_bury_note),
 53→    SUSPEND_NOTE(R.string.menu_suspend_note),
 54→    TOGGLE_FLAG_RED(R.string.gesture_flag_red),
 55→    TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE(R.string.gesture_flag_orange),
 56→    TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN(R.string.gesture_flag_green),
 57→    TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE(R.string.gesture_flag_blue),
 58→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK(R.string.gesture_flag_pink),
 59→    TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE(R.string.gesture_flag_turquoise),
 60→    TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE(R.string.gesture_flag_purple),
 61→    UNSET_FLAG(R.string.gesture_flag_remove),
 62→    PAGE_UP(R.string.gesture_page_up),
 63→    PAGE_DOWN(R.string.gesture_page_down),
 64→    TAG(R.string.add_tag),
 65→    CARD_INFO(R.string.card_info_title),
 66→    ABORT_AND_SYNC(R.string.gesture_abort_sync),
 67→    RECORD_VOICE(R.string.record_voice),
 68→    SAVE_VOICE(R.string.save_voice),
 69→    REPLAY_VOICE(R.string.replay_voice),
 70→    TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD(R.string.gesture_toggle_whiteboard),
 71→    CLEAR_WHITEBOARD(R.string.clear_whiteboard),
 72→    CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR(R.string.title_whiteboard_editor),
 73→    SHOW_HINT(R.string.gesture_show_hint),
 74→    SHOW_ALL_HINTS(R.string.gesture_show_all_hints),
 75→    ADD_NOTE(R.string.menu_add_note),
 76→    RESCHEDULE_NOTE(R.string.card_editor_reschedule_card);
 77→
 78→    companion object {
 79→        val allDefaultBindings: List<MappableBinding>
 80→            get() = Arrays.stream(entries.toTypedArray())
 81→                .flatMap { x: ViewerCommand -> x.defaultValue.stream() }
 82→                .collect(Collectors.toList())
 83→
 84→        fun fromPreferenceKey(key: String) = ViewerCommand.entries.first { it.preferenceKey == key }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    val preferenceKey: String
 88→        get() = "binding_$name"
 89→
 90→    fun addBinding(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
 91→        val addAtStart = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
 92→            // reorder the elements, moving the added binding to the first position
 93→            collection.remove(element)
 94→            collection.add(0, element)
 95→            true
 96→        }
 97→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtStart)
 98→    }
 99→
100→    fun addBindingAtEnd(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
101→        val addAtEnd = BiFunction { collection: MutableList<MappableBinding>, element: MappableBinding ->
102→            // do not reorder the elements
103→            if (collection.contains(element)) {
104→                return@BiFunction false
105→            }
106→            collection.add(element)
107→            return@BiFunction true
108→        }
109→        addBindingInternal(preferences, binding, addAtEnd)
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun addBindingInternal(preferences: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding, performAdd: BiFunction<MutableList<MappableBinding>, MappableBinding, Boolean>) {
113→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = fromPreference(preferences, this)
114→        performAdd.apply(bindings, binding)
115→        val newValue: String = bindings.toPreferenceString()
116→        preferences.edit { putString(preferenceKey, newValue) }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun removeBinding(prefs: SharedPreferences, binding: MappableBinding) {
120→        val bindings: MutableList<MappableBinding> = MappableBinding.fromPreferenceString(preferenceKey)
121→        bindings.remove(binding)
122→        prefs.edit {
123→            putString(preferenceKey, bindings.toPreferenceString())
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    // If we use the serialised format, then this adds additional coupling to the properties.
128→    val defaultValue: List<MappableBinding>
129→        get() {
130→            // all of the default commands are currently for the Reviewer
131→            fun keyCode(keycode: Int, side: CardSide, modifierKeys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()) =
132→                keyCode(keycode, Screen.Reviewer(side), modifierKeys)
133→            fun unicode(c: Char, side: CardSide) = unicode(c, Screen.Reviewer(side))
134→            return when (this) {
135→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1 -> listOf(
136→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_Y, CardSide.BOTH),
137→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.ANSWER),
138→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.ANSWER)
139→                )
140→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2 -> listOf(
141→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_X, CardSide.BOTH),
142→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.ANSWER),
143→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.ANSWER)
144→                )
145→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3 -> listOf(
146→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_B, CardSide.BOTH),
147→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
148→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.ANSWER),
149→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_DPAD_CENTER, CardSide.BOTH),
150→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_SPACE, CardSide.ANSWER),
151→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER),
152→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_ENTER, CardSide.ANSWER)
153→                )
154→                FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4 -> listOf(
155→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_BUTTON_A, CardSide.BOTH),
156→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.ANSWER),
157→                    keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.ANSWER)
158→                )
159→                EDIT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_E, CardSide.BOTH))
160→                MARK -> listOf(unicode('*', CardSide.BOTH))
161→                BURY_CARD -> listOf(unicode('-', CardSide.BOTH))
162→                BURY_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('=', CardSide.BOTH))
163→                SUSPEND_CARD -> listOf(unicode('@', CardSide.BOTH))
164→                SUSPEND_NOTE -> listOf(unicode('!', CardSide.BOTH))
165→                PLAY_MEDIA -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_R, CardSide.BOTH), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_F5, CardSide.BOTH))
166→                REPLAY_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH))
167→                RECORD_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_V, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
168→                SAVE_VOICE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_S, CardSide.BOTH, shift()))
169→                UNDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH))
170→                REDO -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_Z, CardSide.BOTH, ModifierKeys(shift = true, ctrl = true, alt = false)))
171→                TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_1, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
172→                TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_2, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
173→                TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_3, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
174→                TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_4, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
175→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_5, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
176→                TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_6, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
177→                TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()), keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_NUMPAD_7, CardSide.BOTH, ctrl()))
178→                SHOW_HINT -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_H, CardSide.BOTH))
179→                SHOW_ALL_HINTS -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_G, CardSide.BOTH))
180→                ADD_NOTE -> listOf(keyCode(KeyEvent.KEYCODE_A, CardSide.BOTH))
181→                else -> emptyList()
182→            }
183→        }
184→
185→    private fun keyCode(keycode: Int, screen: Screen, keys: ModifierKeys = ModifierKeys.none()): MappableBinding {
186→        return MappableBinding(keyCode(keys, keycode), screen)
187→    }
188→
189→    private fun unicode(c: Char, screen: Screen): MappableBinding {
190→        return MappableBinding(unicode(c), screen)
191→    }
192→
193→    fun interface CommandProcessor {
194→        /**
195→         * @return whether the command was executed
196→         */
197→        fun executeCommand(which: ViewerCommand, fromGesture: Gesture?): Boolean
198→    }
199→}

 279→    private fun selectDeckFromExtra() {
 280→        val extras = intent.extras
 281→        if (extras == null || !extras.containsKey("deckId")) {
 282→            // deckId is not set, load default
 283→            return
 284→        }
 285→        val did = extras.getLong("deckId", Long.MIN_VALUE)
 286→        Timber.d("selectDeckFromExtra() with deckId = %d", did)
 287→
 288→        // deckId does not exist, load default
 289→        if (getColUnsafe.decks.get(did) == null) {
 290→            Timber.w("selectDeckFromExtra() deckId '%d' doesn't exist", did)
 291→            return
 292→        }
 293→        // Select the deck
 294→        getColUnsafe.decks.select(did)
 295→    }

 309→    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: Collection) {
 310→        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
 311→        if (Intent.ACTION_VIEW == intent.action) {
 312→            Timber.d("onCreate() :: received Intent with action = %s", intent.action)
 313→            selectDeckFromExtra()
 314→        }
 315→        // Load the first card and start reviewing. Uses the answer card
 316→        // task to load a card, but since we send null
 317→        // as the card to answer, no card will be answered.
 318→        prefWhiteboard = MetaDB.getWhiteboardState(this, parentDid)
 319→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 320→            // DEFECT: Slight inefficiency here, as we set the database using these methods
 321→            val whiteboardVisibility = MetaDB.getWhiteboardVisibility(this, parentDid)
 322→            setWhiteboardEnabledState(true)
 323→            setWhiteboardVisibility(whiteboardVisibility)
 324→            toggleStylus = MetaDB.getWhiteboardStylusState(this, parentDid)
 325→            whiteboard!!.toggleStylus = toggleStylus
 326→        }
 327→        launchCatchingTask {
 328→            withCol { startTimebox() }
 329→            updateCardAndRedraw()
 330→        }
 331→        disableDrawerSwipeOnConflicts()
 332→
 333→        // Set full screen/immersive mode if needed
 334→        if (prefFullscreenReview) {
 335→            setFullScreen(this)
 336→        }
 337→        setRenderWorkaround(this)
 338→    }

 593→                this,
 594→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),

 596→            )

 602→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 603→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 604→        if (isMicToolBarVisible) {
 605→            micToolBarLayer.visibility = View.GONE
 606→        } else {
 607→            setReviewerStatus(false)
 608→            if (!isAudioUIInitialized) {
 609→                try {
 610→                    audioRecordingController = AudioRecordingController()
 611→                    audioRecordingController?.createUI(this, micToolBarLayer)
 612→                } catch (e: Exception) {
 613→                    Timber.w(e, "unable to add the audio recorder to toolbar")
 614→                    CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "Unable to create recorder tool bar")
 615→                    showThemedToast(
 616→                        this,
 617→                        this.getText(R.string.multimedia_editor_audio_view_create_failed).toString(),
 618→                        true
 619→                    )
 620→                }
 621→                isAudioUIInitialized = true
 622→            }
 623→            micToolBarLayer.visibility = View.VISIBLE
 624→        }
 625→        isMicToolBarVisible = !isMicToolBarVisible
 626→    }

 689→    // Related to https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/11061#issuecomment-1107868455
 690→    @NeedsTest("Order of operations needs Testing around Menu (Overflow) Icons and their colors.")
 691→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 692→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 693→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 694→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 695→        displayIcons(menu)
 696→        actionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 697→        val alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 698→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)
 699→        if (currentCard != null && isMarked(getColUnsafe, currentCard!!.note(getColUnsafe))) {
 700→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_unmark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_white)
 701→        } else {
 702→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_mark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_border_white)
 703→        }
 704→        markCardIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 705→
 706→        val flagIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_flag)
 707→        if (flagIcon != null) {
 708→            if (currentCard != null) {
 709→                when (currentCard!!.userFlag()) {
 710→                    1 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_red)
 711→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 712→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 713→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 714→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 715→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 716→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 717→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)
 718→                }
 719→            }
 720→            flagIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 721→        }
 722→
 723→        // Undo button
 724→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int
 725→        val undoEnabled: Boolean
 726→        val whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes = showWhiteboard && whiteboard?.undoEmpty() == false
 727→        if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 728→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser
 729→            undoEnabled = true
 730→        } else {
 731→            undoIconId = R.drawable.ic_undo_white
 732→            undoEnabled = colIsOpenUnsafe() && getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()
 733→        }
 734→        val alphaUndo = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 735→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)
 736→        undoIcon.setIcon(undoIconId)
 737→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 738→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled
 739→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe()) { // Required mostly because there are tests where `col` is null
 740→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 741→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo_action_whiteboard_last_stroke)
 742→            } else if (getColUnsafe.undoAvailable()) {
 743→                undoIcon.title = getColUnsafe.undoLabel()
 744→                //  e.g. Undo Bury, Undo Change Deck, Undo Update Note
 745→            } else {
 746→                // In this case, there is no object word for the verb, "Undo",
 747→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,
 748→                // we need to use the string for just "Undo" instead of the string for "Undo %s".
 749→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo)
 750→                undoIcon.iconAlpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 751→            }
 752→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_redo)?.apply {
 753→                if (getColUnsafe.redoAvailable()) {
 754→                    title = getColUnsafe.redoLabel()
 755→                    iconAlpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 756→                    isEnabled = true
 757→                } else {
 758→                    setTitle(R.string.redo)
 759→                    iconAlpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 760→                    isEnabled = false
 761→                }
 762→            }
 763→        }
 764→        if (undoEnabled) {
 765→            onboarding.onUndoButtonEnabled()
 766→        }
 767→        val toggleWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard)
 768→        val toggleStylusIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_stylus)
 769→        val hideWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_hide_whiteboard)
 770→        val changePenColorIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color)
 771→        // White board button
 772→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 773→            // Configure the whiteboard related items in the action bar
 774→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_whiteboard)
 775→            // Always allow "Disable Whiteboard", even if "Enable Whiteboard" is disabled
 776→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 777→            if (!actionButtons.status.toggleStylusIsDisabled()) {
 778→                toggleStylusIcon.isVisible = true
 779→            }
 780→            if (!actionButtons.status.hideWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 781→                hideWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 782→            }
 783→            if (!actionButtons.status.clearWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 784→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_clear_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 785→            }
 786→            if (!actionButtons.status.saveWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 787→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_save_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 788→            }
 789→            if (!actionButtons.status.whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled()) {
 790→                changePenColorIcon.isVisible = true
 791→            }
 792→            val whiteboardIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_white)!!.mutate()
 793→            val stylusIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_stylus)!!.mutate()
 794→            val whiteboardColorPaletteIcon = VectorDrawableCompat.create(resources, R.drawable.ic_color_lens_white_24dp, this.theme)!!.mutate()
 795→            if (showWhiteboard) {
 796→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 797→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 798→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.hide_whiteboard)
 799→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 800→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 801→                if (toggleStylus) {
 802→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_stylus)
 803→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 804→                } else {
 805→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_stylus)
 806→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 807→                }
 808→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 809→            } else {
 810→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 811→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 812→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.show_whiteboard)
 813→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 814→                stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 815→                toggleStylusIcon.isEnabled = false
 816→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 817→                changePenColorIcon.isEnabled = false
 818→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 819→                colorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 820→            }
 821→        } else {
 822→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_whiteboard)
 823→        }
 824→        if (colIsOpenUnsafe() && getColUnsafe.decks.isDyn(parentDid)) {
 825→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_open_deck_options).isVisible = false
 826→        }
 827→        if (tts.enabled && !actionButtons.status.selectTtsIsDisabled()) {
 828→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_select_tts).isVisible = true
 829→        }
 830→        if (!suspendNoteAvailable() && !actionButtons.status.suspendIsDisabled()) {
 831→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_suspend).isVisible = false
 832→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_suspend_card).isVisible = true
 833→        }
 834→        if (!buryNoteAvailable() && !actionButtons.status.buryIsDisabled()) {
 835→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_bury).isVisible = false
 836→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_bury_card).isVisible = true
 837→        }
 838→
 839→        onboarding.onCreate()
 840→
 841→        increaseHorizontalPaddingOfOverflowMenuIcons(menu)
 842→        tintOverflowMenuIcons(menu, skipIf = { isFlagResource(it.itemId) })

 844→        return super.onCreateOptionsMenu(menu)

 897→    override fun displayAnswerBottomBar() {
 898→        super.displayAnswerBottomBar()
 899→        onboarding.onAnswerShown()
 900→        // Set correct label and background resource for each button
 901→        // Note that it's necessary to set the resource dynamically as the ease2 / ease3 buttons
 902→        // (which libanki expects ease to be 2 and 3) can either be hard, good, or easy - depending on num buttons shown
 903→        val background = getBackgroundColors(this)
 904→        val textColor = getTextColors(this)
 905→        easeButton1!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 906→        easeButton1!!.setColor(background[0])
 907→        easeButton4!!.setColor(background[3])
 908→        // Ease 2 is "hard"
 909→        easeButton2!!.setup(background[1], textColor[1], R.string.ease_button_hard)
 910→        easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 911→        // Ease 3 is good
 912→        easeButton3!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 913→        easeButton4!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 914→        easeButton3!!.requestFocus()
 915→
 916→        // Show next review time
 917→        if (shouldShowNextReviewTime()) {
 918→            val state = queueState!!
 919→            launchCatchingTask {
 920→                val labels = withCol { sched.describeNextStates(state.states) }
 921→                easeButton1!!.nextTime = labels[0]
 922→                easeButton2!!.nextTime = labels[1]
 923→                easeButton3!!.nextTime = labels[2]
 924→                easeButton4!!.nextTime = labels[3]
 925→            }
 926→        }
 927→    }

 951→    private fun updateWhiteboardEditorPosition() {

 953→            .getString("answerButtonPosition", "bottom")
 954→        val layoutParams: RelativeLayout.LayoutParams
 955→        when (answerButtonsPosition) {
 956→            "none", "top" -> {
 957→                layoutParams = colorPalette.layoutParams as RelativeLayout.LayoutParams
 958→                layoutParams.removeRule(RelativeLayout.ABOVE)
 959→                layoutParams.addRule(RelativeLayout.ALIGN_PARENT_BOTTOM)
 960→                colorPalette.layoutParams = layoutParams
 961→            }
 962→

 972→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
 973→        val queue = queueState ?: return
 974→        super.updateActionBar()
 975→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
 976→        val counts = queue.counts
 977→        if (actionBar != null) {
 978→            if (prefShowETA) {
 979→                launchCatchingTask {
 980→                    eta = withCol { sched.eta(counts, false) }
 981→                    actionBar.subtitle = remainingTime(this@Reviewer, (eta * 60).toLong())
 982→                }
 983→            }
 984→        }
 985→        newCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
 986→        lrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
 987→        revCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
 988→        if (prefHideDueCount) {
 989→            revCount = SpannableString("???")
 990→        }
 991→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
 992→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
 993→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
 994→            when (queue.countsIndex) {
 995→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> newCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, newCount!!.length, 0)
 996→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> lrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, lrnCount!!.length, 0)
 997→                Counts.Queue.REV -> revCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, revCount!!.length, 0)
 998→            }
 999→        }
1000→        textBarNew.text = newCount
1001→        textBarLearn.text = lrnCount
1002→        textBarReview.text = revCount
1003→    }

1021→    override suspend fun updateCurrentCard() {
1022→        val state = withCol {
1023→            sched.currentQueueState()?.apply {
1024→                topCard.renderOutput(true)
1025→            }
1026→        }
1027→        state?.timeboxReached?.let { dealWithTimeBox(it) }

1095→    private fun runStateMutationHook() {
1096→        val state = queueState ?: return
1097→        if (state.customSchedulingJs.isEmpty()) {
1098→            statesMutated = true
1099→            return
1100→        }
1101→        val key = customSchedulingKey
1102→        val js = state.customSchedulingJs
1103→        webView?.evaluateJavascript(
1104→            """
1105→        anki.mutateNextCardStates('$key', async (states, customData, ctx) => { $js })
1106→            .catch(err => { console.log(err); window.location.href = "state-mutation-error:"; });
1107→"""
1108→        ) { result ->
1109→            if ("null" == result) {
1110→                // eval failed, usually a syntax error
1111→                // Note, we get "null" (string) and not null
1112→                statesMutated = true
1113→            }
1114→        }
1115→    }

1291→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (

1293→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN

1295→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1296→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1297→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1298→            )
1299→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1300→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1301→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1302→        a.window.statusBarColor = MaterialColors.getColor(a, R.attr.appBarColor, 0)
1303→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1304→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1305→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1306→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1307→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1308→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1309→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1310→            }
1311→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1312→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1313→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1314→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1315→            if (visible) {
1316→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1317→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1318→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1319→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1320→                }
1321→            } else {
1322→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1323→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1324→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1325→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1326→                }
1327→            }
1328→        }

1353→    override suspend fun handlePostRequest(uri: String, bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1354→        return if (uri.startsWith(ANKI_PREFIX)) {
1355→            when (val methodName = uri.substring(ANKI_PREFIX.length)) {
1356→                "getSchedulingStatesWithContext" -> getSchedulingStatesWithContext()
1357→                "setSchedulingStates" -> setSchedulingStates(bytes)
1358→                else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $methodName")
1359→            }
1360→        } else if (uri.startsWith(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX)) {
1361→            jsApi.handleJsApiRequest(
1362→                uri.substring(ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX.length),
1363→                bytes,
1364→                returnDefaultValues = false
1365→            )
1366→        } else {
1367→            throw IllegalArgumentException("unhandled request: $uri")
1368→        }
1369→    }

1371→    private fun getSchedulingStatesWithContext(): ByteArray {
1372→        val state = queueState ?: return ByteArray(0)
1373→        return state.schedulingStatesWithContext().toBuilder()
1374→            .mergeStates(
1375→                state.states.toBuilder().mergeCurrent(
1376→                    state.states.current.toBuilder()
1377→                        .setCustomData(state.topCard.toBackendCard().customData).build()
1378→                ).build()
1379→            )
1380→            .build()
1381→            .toByteArray()
1382→    }

1384→    private fun setSchedulingStates(bytes: ByteArray): ByteArray {
1385→        val state = queueState
1386→        if (state == null) {
1387→            statesMutated = true
1388→            return ByteArray(0)
1389→        }
1390→        val req = SetSchedulingStatesRequest.parseFrom(bytes)
1391→        if (req.key == customSchedulingKey) {
1392→            state.states = req.states
1393→        }
1394→        statesMutated = true
1395→        return ByteArray(0)
1396→    }

1398→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1399→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1400→
1401→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1402→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1403→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1404→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1405→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1406→        }
1407→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1408→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1409→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1410→            }
1411→        })
1412→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1413→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1414→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1415→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1416→                !showWhiteboard || (
1417→                    prefFullscreenReview &&
1418→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1419→                    )
1420→                )
1421→            ) {
1422→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1423→                v.performClick()
1424→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1425→            }
1426→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1427→        }
1428→    }

 46→object PreferenceUpgradeService {
 47→    fun upgradePreferences(context: Context, previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): Boolean =
 48→        upgradePreferences(context.sharedPrefs(), previousVersionCode)
 49→
 50→    /** @return Whether any preferences were upgraded */
 51→    internal fun upgradePreferences(preferences: SharedPreferences, previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): Boolean {
 52→        val pendingPreferenceUpgrades = PreferenceUpgrade.getPendingUpgrades(preferences, previousVersionCode)
 53→
 54→        pendingPreferenceUpgrades.forEach {
 55→            it.performUpgrade(preferences)
 56→        }
 57→
 58→        return pendingPreferenceUpgrades.isNotEmpty()
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    /**
 62→     * Specifies that no preference upgrades need to happen.
 63→     * Typically because the app has been run for the first time, or the preferences
 64→     * have been deleted
 65→     */
 66→    @JvmStatic // required for mockito for now
 67→    fun setPreferencesUpToDate(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
 68→        Timber.i("Marking preferences as up to date")
 69→        PreferenceUpgrade.setPreferenceToLatestVersion(preferences)
 70→    }

 72→    abstract class PreferenceUpgrade private constructor(val versionIdentifier: VersionIdentifier) {

 74→        To add a new preference upgrade:
 75→          * yield a new class from getAllInstances (do not use `legacyPreviousVersionCode` in the constructor)
 76→          * Implement the upgrade() method
 77→          * Set mVersionIdentifier to 1 more than the previous versionIdentifier
 78→          * Run tests in PreferenceUpgradeServiceTest
 79→         */
 80→
 81→        companion object {
 82→            /** A version code where the value doesn't matter as we're not using the result */
 83→            private const val IGNORED_LEGACY_VERSION_CODE = 0L
 84→            const val upgradeVersionPrefKey = "preferenceUpgradeVersion"
 85→
 86→            /** Returns all instances of preference upgrade classes */
 87→            private fun getAllInstances(legacyPreviousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier) = sequence {
 88→                yield(LegacyPreferenceUpgrade(legacyPreviousVersionCode))
 89→                yield(UpdateNoteEditorToolbarPrefs())
 90→                yield(UpgradeGesturesToControls())
 91→                yield(UpgradeDayAndNightThemes())
 92→                yield(UpgradeFetchMedia())
 93→                yield(UpgradeAppLocale())
 94→                yield(RemoveScrollingButtons())
 95→                yield(RemoveAnswerRecommended())
 96→                yield(RemoveBackupMax())
 97→                yield(RemoveInCardsMode())
 98→                yield(RemoveReviewerETA())
 99→                yield(SetShowDeckTitle())
100→                yield(ResetAnalyticsOptIn())
101→            }
102→
103→            /** Returns a list of preference upgrade classes which have not been applied */
104→            fun getPendingUpgrades(preferences: SharedPreferences, legacyPreviousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier): List<PreferenceUpgrade> {
105→                val currentPrefVersion: VersionIdentifier = getPreferenceVersion(preferences)
106→
107→                return getAllInstances(legacyPreviousVersionCode).filter {
108→                    it.versionIdentifier > currentPrefVersion
109→                }.toList()
110→            }
111→
112→            /** Sets the preference version such that no upgrades need to be applied */
113→            fun setPreferenceToLatestVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
114→                val versionWhichRequiresNoUpgrades = getLatestVersion()
115→                setPreferenceVersion(preferences, versionWhichRequiresNoUpgrades)
116→            }
117→
118→            internal fun getPreferenceVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences) =
119→                preferences.getInt(upgradeVersionPrefKey, 0)
120→
121→            internal fun setPreferenceVersion(preferences: SharedPreferences, versionIdentifier: VersionIdentifier) {
122→                Timber.i("upgrading preference version to '$versionIdentifier'")
123→                preferences.edit { putInt(upgradeVersionPrefKey, versionIdentifier) }
124→            }
125→
126→            /** Returns the collection of all preference version numbers */
127→            @VisibleForTesting
128→            fun getAllVersionIdentifiers(): Sequence<VersionIdentifier> =
129→                getAllInstances(IGNORED_LEGACY_VERSION_CODE).map { it.versionIdentifier }
130→
131→            /**
132→             * @return the latest "version" of the preferences
133→             * If the preferences are set to this version, then no upgrades will take place
134→             */
135→            private fun getLatestVersion(): VersionIdentifier = getAllVersionIdentifiers().maxOrNull() ?: 0
136→        }
137→
138→        /** Handles preference upgrades before 2021-08-01,
139→         * upgrades were detected via a version code comparison
140→         * rather than comparing a preference value
141→         */
142→        private class LegacyPreferenceUpgrade(val previousVersionCode: LegacyVersionIdentifier) : PreferenceUpgrade(1) {
143→            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
144→                if (!needsLegacyPreferenceUpgrade(previousVersionCode)) {
145→                    return
146→                }
147→
148→                Timber.i("running upgradePreferences()")
149→                // clear all prefs if super old version to prevent any errors
150→                if (previousVersionCode < 20300130) {
151→                    Timber.i("Old version of Anki - Clearing preferences")
152→                    preferences.edit { clear() }
153→                }
154→                // when upgrading from before 2.5alpha35
155→                if (previousVersionCode < 20500135) {
156→                    Timber.i("Old version of Anki - Fixing Zoom")
157→                    // Card zooming behaviour was changed the preferences renamed
158→                    val oldCardZoom = preferences.getInt("relativeDisplayFontSize", 100)
159→                    val oldImageZoom = preferences.getInt("relativeImageSize", 100)
160→                    preferences.edit {
161→                        putInt("cardZoom", oldCardZoom)
162→                        putInt("imageZoom", oldImageZoom)
163→                    }
164→                    if (!preferences.getBoolean("useBackup", true)) {
165→                        preferences.edit { putInt("backupMax", 0) }
166→                    }
167→                    preferences.edit {
168→                        remove("useBackup")
169→                        remove("intentAdditionInstantAdd")
170→                    }
171→                }
172→                FullScreenMode.upgradeFromLegacyPreference(preferences)
173→            }
174→
175→            fun needsLegacyPreferenceUpgrade(previous: Long): Boolean = previous < CHECK_PREFERENCES_AT_VERSION
176→
177→            companion object {
178→                /**
179→                 * The latest package version number that included changes to the preferences that requires handling. All
180→                 * collections being upgraded to (or after) this version must update preferences.
181→                 *
182→                 * #9309 Do not modify this variable - it no longer works.
183→                 *
184→                 * Instead, add an unconditional check for the old preference before the call to
185→                 * "needsPreferenceUpgrade", and perform the upgrade.
186→                 */
187→                const val CHECK_PREFERENCES_AT_VERSION = 20500225
188→            }
189→        }
190→
191→        fun performUpgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
192→            Timber.i("Running preference upgrade: ${this.javaClass.simpleName}")
193→            upgrade(preferences)
194→
195→            setPreferenceVersion(preferences, this.versionIdentifier)
196→        }
197→
198→        protected abstract fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences)
199→
200→        /**
201→         * update toolbar buttons with new preferences, when button text empty or null then it adds the index as button text
202→         */
203→        internal class UpdateNoteEditorToolbarPrefs : PreferenceUpgrade(4) {
204→            override fun upgrade(preferences: SharedPreferences) {
205→                val buttons = getNewToolbarButtons(preferences)
206→
207→                // update prefs
208→                preferences.edit {
209→                    remove("note_editor_custom_buttons")
210→                    putStringSet("note_editor_custom_buttons", CustomToolbarButton.toStringSet(buttons))
211→                }
212→            }
213→
214→            private fun getNewToolbarButtons(preferences: SharedPreferences): ArrayList<CustomToolbarButton> {
215→                // get old toolbar prefs
216→                val set = preferences.getStringSet("note_editor_custom_buttons", hashSetInit<String>(0)) as Set<String?>
217→                // new list with buttons size
218→                val buttons = ArrayList<CustomToolbarButton>(set.size)
219→
220→                // parse fields with separator
221→                for (s in set) {
222→                    val fields = s!!.split(Consts.FIELD_SEPARATOR.toRegex(), CustomToolbarButton.KEEP_EMPTY_ENTRIES.coerceAtLeast(0)).toTypedArray()
223→                    if (fields.size != 3) {
224→                        continue
225→                    }
226→
227→                    val index: Int = try {
228→                        fields[0].toInt()
229→                    } catch (e: Exception) {
230→                        Timber.w(e)
231→                        continue
232→                    }
233→
234→                    // add new button with the index + 1 as button text
235→                    val visualIndex: Int = index + 1
236→                    val buttonText = visualIndex.toString()
237→
238→                    // fields 1 is prefix, fields 2 is suffix
239→                    buttons.add(CustomToolbarButton(index, buttonText, fields[1], fields[2]))
240→                }
241→                return buttons
242→            }
243→        }
244→
245→        internal class UpgradeGesturesToControls : PreferenceUpgrade(5) {
246→            val oldCommandValues = mapOf(
247→                Pair(1, ViewerCommand.SHOW_ANSWER),
248→                Pair(2, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1),
249→                Pair(3, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2),
250→                Pair(4, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3),
251→                Pair(5, ViewerCommand.FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4),
252→                Pair(8, ViewerCommand.UNDO),
253→                Pair(9, ViewerCommand.EDIT),
254→                Pair(10, ViewerCommand.MARK),
255→                Pair(12, ViewerCommand.BURY_CARD),
256→                Pair(13, ViewerCommand.SUSPEND_CARD),
257→                Pair(14, ViewerCommand.DELETE),
258→                Pair(16, ViewerCommand.PLAY_MEDIA),
259→                Pair(17, ViewerCommand.EXIT),
260→                Pair(18, ViewerCommand.BURY_NOTE),

 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig

 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analytics_opt_in"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }
264→
265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

292→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
293→    fun resetForTests() {
294→        sAnalytics = null
295→    }

297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }
